purpose: Bounded covering-array schedule for the next hard-part fixture wave. The repo already has rich fixture packs and runtime_math covering-array machinery; this manifest adds an explicit *interaction* contract on top of them so future hard-part work picks high-order combinations of (mode, symbol family, errno edge, locale, threading regime, allocator pressure, workload class) without re-deriving the schedule and without exploding test cost. The schedule is pairwise (t=2): every pair of factor values must appear in at least one vector, and the harness checker fails closed if any pair is missing.

mode.rationale: The two production runtime modes from runtime_policy.

family.rationale: Highest-volume ABI families with hard-part interaction risk: stdio I/O, malloc allocator pressure, pthread concurrency, string memory boundaries, locale UTF-8 codec switching.

errno.rationale: Success plus three errno edges that exercise distinct healing paths (bad pointer, bad arg, allocator failure).

locale_setting.rationale: C anchors the no-multibyte path; en_US.UTF-8 is the dominant UTF-8 locale; ja_JP.UTF-8 stresses 3-byte CJK sequences that often trip locale-aware comparators.

threading.rationale: Single-threaded baseline, low-concurrency contention sentinel, and 64-core swarm-realistic stress.

allocator_pressure.rationale: Low pressure exercises the fast path; high pressure forces fallback / reclamation paths and overlaps with ENOMEM at the symbol layer.

workload_class.rationale: hotpath claims must hit the strict <20ns / hardened <200ns budgets; coldpath claims do not — interactions still need to verify the runtime distinguishes them.

coverage_strength_rationale: Pairwise (t=2) is empirically known to catch the majority of multi-factor defects at a small fraction of full-factorial cost (185 pairs vs 1440 vectors). t=3 (3-wise) is the upgrade path; this manifest names the upgrade trigger but does not yet enumerate t=3 vectors.
upgrade_trigger_t3: If any closed bead in the bd-juvqm.* family reproduces a defect that requires a 3-way factor combination not covered by the t=2 schedule (i.e., the schedule's vectors all pass but a hand-derived 3-tuple fails), regenerate this manifest at coverage_strength=3 and bump schema_version to v2.

downstream_fixture_families.rationale: errno-precedence fuzz is the next family that will benefit from a deduped schedule covering errno × family × allocator_pressure interactions.
locale × threading interactions trip the per-thread locale state machine in iconv; this schedule provides the interaction set.
Baseline manifest will sample p50/p95/p99/p999 across these factors using the tail_stats library (bd-juvqm.11) for confidence intervals.

skipped_combinations.rationale: Hardened mode cannot return ENOMEM under low allocator pressure without an injection harness. The injection harness is intentionally out of scope for this schedule; bd-juvqm.7 (evidence ring backpressure stress) covers the injection path separately.

regeneration_note: When any factor's `values` list changes, regenerate the vectors with a greedy IPOG-equivalent solver and update `expected_pair_count`, `expected_vector_count`, and `vectors`. Do not hand-edit individual vectors — the harness checker enforces every pair is covered, so a hand-edit that drops a pair fails closed.
